comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Verified in current checkout as a user-facing error formatting issue. extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/index.ts:73-84 wraps registry/validation failures with AgentMenuRegistryLoadError using reason: Cause.pretty(cause). formatRegistryLoadError(...) then shows that reason directly in notifications at lines 92-111. Cause.pretty can include verbose internal structure from Effect causes rather than a concise actionable message. This is not a correctness bug in registry loading itself, but the review claim is accurate that UI notifications currently expose raw pretty-printed causes instead of intentionally mapped user-facing errors.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Switched agents-menu registry-load error mapping from `Cause.pretty(...)` to concise message extraction. In `extensions/tau/src/agents-menu/index.ts`, `loadRegistry(...)` now catches typed failures directly and stores a summarized user-facing reason (`error.message` when available, otherwise a short fallback) in `AgentMenuRegistryLoadError`. This removes stack/trace formatting leakage from UI notifications while preserving actionable validation text (e.g. invalid tools). Added/strengthened regression assertion in `extensions/tau/test/agents-menu.test.ts` (`fails closed on visible session_start when registry loading fails`) to require concise single-line notification content containing the validation reason and no newline stack output. Validation: `npm run gate` passed.
